card.title: mazer: cross-viewport UI reliability and layout hardening
card.summary: Coordinate mobile safe-area, shared overlay geometry, and browser layout through one no-overlap contract and proof matrix.
card.objective: Make menu, Options, play, and Pause remain readable and operable across phone, browser, PWA, and restored-window surfaces without per-screenshot offsets.
acceptanceCriteria: Every primary Mazer surface has an owner, supported viewport category, and no-overlap contract.
Mobile and browser layout work can be verified independently and together through a shared matrix.
No completion claim relies on desktop-only proof for a phone or restored-window defect.
Physical-device findings remain open until operator screenshots confirm the deployed behavior.
discoveries: Operator production screenshots correctly rejected the earlier review evidence because status, guide, and action text still visibly touched or crossed chrome.
Logical Phaser bounds under-reported physical painted text width, so the proof contract now requires stronger insets and excludes text from active scroll-fade bands.
nextActions: Review commit c7b1540e on a physical iPhone using the exact corrected preview and attach menu, Options, play, and Pause screenshots.
Keep native browser chrome and maximize or restore proof open until physical evidence passes.
evidence: Mazer draft PR #76 at head c7b1540e.
Focused visible-text verification passed 62 tests; full verification passed 363 tests and the production build.
Preview dpl_FGKmMPHb64mGqdGykzQAmZ6YspNX passed exact deployed 393x852 containment, fade-clearance, overlap, and console checks.

entry.headline: Operator-visible text bleed corrected on the exact PR 76 preview
completed: Added physical safety insets to shared run-status, guide, toggle, and action text.
Prevented partial guide or row text from rendering beneath active scroll fades.
Re-proved the exact deployed 393x852 menu, Options, play, and Pause surfaces.
discovered: The previous automated pass was insufficient because it measured logical bounds more optimistically than the phone painted glyphs.
next: Collect operator iPhone screenshots from the corrected preview before production promotion or completion.

card.title: mazer: normalize browser UI layout across viewports
card.summary: Normalize responsive browser geometry and preserve only semantic settings so viewport changes cannot replay stale layout.
card.objective: Recompute all menu, Options, play, and Pause geometry from the active content rectangle while preserving explicit player preferences only.
acceptanceCriteria: Layout-derived geometry is never persisted as player preference state.
A recalculated layout cannot overlap, escape the viewport, or hide essential controls.
Normal reload preserves supported semantic settings with safe migration defaults.
Narrow, wide, phone, and restore endpoints have deterministic evidence.
discoveries: A logical bounds pass did not guarantee that antialiased physical glyphs stayed visually clear of the panel rails.
Scroll fades must reserve an explicit no-text render viewport rather than merely overlaying alpha on clipped content.
nextActions: Complete physical iPhone review of the corrected c7b1540e preview.
Retain native browser chrome and maximize or restore as separate remaining evidence.
evidence: Exact 393x852 deployed preview proof passed with no text under scroll fades and no console or page errors.

entry.headline: Physical text containment and scroll-fade clearance strengthened
completed: Reserved fade-safe scroll render bands for guide and settings content.
Applied fitted typography to every overlay and menu action button.
Added deployed checks for action-label insets and scroll-fade text clearance.
discovered: Production screenshots remained on the older release while the corrected candidate was still in preview, which made the edit appear absent.
next: Use operator physical screenshots to decide whether the corrected candidate may advance to production.

card.title: mazer: shared menu and play run status panel
card.summary: Use one responsive menu and play status component for time, Rank or AI Rank, Score out of 100, and Maze level from the correct context.
card.objective: Give menu and active play the same compact status language while keeping score authority traceable to the canonical scorer.
acceptanceCriteria: Menu and play consume the same status-panel formatting and layout contract.
Panel text remains centered, contained, and readable at phone and desktop widths.
Panel width, gap, and play Pause placement satisfy shared viewport diagnostics.
Displayed score is traceable to canonical run-quality data rather than a presentation-only calculation.
discoveries: The play Maze value visibly touched the right rail even though the previous logical fit calculation passed.
Physical containment needs conservative horizontal padding and a lower glyph-width safety ratio.
nextActions: Review the corrected menu and play status rows on a physical iPhone.
Identify and consume the finalized canonical 0 to 100 scorer field before completion.
evidence: Mazer draft PR #76 at head c7b1540e uses the corrected shared status geometry.
Exact deployed 393x852 menu and play status proof now has explicit rail clearance.
Canonical score authority remains explicitly non-terminal.

entry.headline: Shared status text now keeps physical rail clearance
completed: Increased compact status-panel width and padding while reducing fitted font size.
Strengthened the diagnostic to require horizontal and vertical text insets.
Re-proved menu and play status rows on the exact preview deployment.
discovered: The earlier shallow fit check was not a reliable proxy for painted phone glyph width.
next: Confirm presentation with operator iPhone screenshots, then continue the scorer-owned dependency separately.
